programme.title: The Bill

programme.desc: In Stratford-upon-Avon, Simon and Steve want a stylish garden that mixes modern looks with heritage touches and dramatic twists, suitable for the home town of William Shakespeare. They therefore task Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich to come up with something that's equal part both stylishly modern and with hints of Tudor heritage.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. The first arrival is Suzanne from London, who hopes that mechanical expert Steve Fletcher can bring a decorative wind-up bird cage that once belonged to her late mother back to its former glory. Meanwhile, church wardens Brian Tidswell and David Dickinson bring in a selection of carved angels for wood restorer Will Kirk to repair. Elsewhere, an old Georgian peat bucket and a surgeon's leather case are restored by experts Dominic Chinea and Suzie Fletcher.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final stage of the culinary contest begins, with the four remaining contestants embarking on a culinary adventure of a lifetime on the island of Mauritius, where they will face three extraordinary challenges. The first challenge takes place in the town of Mahebourg, one of the island's earliest established fishing ports. Split into teams of two, each pair will be tasked with cooking two traditional Mauritian street food dishes for local fishermen. Next, the amateurs travel to the island's capital, Port Louis, where they have the honour of working at the Labourdonnais hotel during a busy lunch service. Finally, the contestants face the daunting challenge of creating a dinner for mentors Chef Moroogun Coopen and Chef Peeroo Nizam, as well as some of the leading figures in Mauritius' food and cultural community.(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Part two of two. Chris Packham investigates the cause behind thousands of birds falling from the sky in the south of America, resulting in panic among residents and predictions of the apocalypse. He also explores the sea foam that gives parts of the Australian coast the appearance of a bubble bath, fish falling from the sky in south London and holes that open in the Earth's crust large enough to swallow buildings, and in Florida even made a lake disappear.(n)

programme.title: The Long Ships (1963)
programme.desc: A Viking warrior steals a ship and leads his crew in search of a legendary bell, rumoured to be three times the size of a man and made of solid gold. But they have reckoned without the ambition of the king of the Moors, who captures the Vikings and intends to use them to find the prize for himself. Adventure, starring Richard Widmark, Sidney Poitier, Rosanna Schiaffino, Russ Tamblyn and Lionel Jeffries.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Nick Davies are at Huddersfield Town Hall to find items to take to auction, while Paul Martin finds out about former prime minister Harold Wilson.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: The host welcomes back mathematician Rachel Riley, singer and farmer JB Gill, newscaster Katie Derham and comedian Richard Herring, who all put their knowledge to the test.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A vet battles to save the life of a pregnant deer and its unborn calf, which is in a dangerous breach position. Jean Johansson finds out how fast a chameleon's tongue can move.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams prepare to leave the checkpoint in Cafayate, Argentina, except for Dom and Lizzie, whose disastrous last leg has left them more than 24 hours behind their competitors. The next destination is the Brazilian island of Ilha Grande, halfway between Rio de Janeiro and Sao Paolo, and at 3,600 kilometres, it is the longest stage of the journey. Emon and Jamiul decide to travel through Paraguay, while Jen and Rob decide they need to see more of the continent, and stop off at the famous Iguazu Falls.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison use virtual reality to redesign a 1930s Surrey house with a poorly designed layout to make better use of the space, and allow the owners to both start a family and have their parents move in with them in the future. Laura's plan involves turning the property into a light-filled interconnected series of spaces, while Robert turns conventional wisdom on its head with a visually striking and flexible design. After experiencing both potential houses, the owners must choose which they would like to put into practice.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a foundry in France that produces iron pots and pans at the rate of one every five seconds, while Cherry Healey is in South Africa, at one of the world's largest iron ore mines, and rides in a giant dumper truck. Historian Ruth Goodman explains how cooking in pots has evolved, from communal ovens to 1970s slow cookers.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Jonathan Ross, Gabby Logan, Mark Wright, Anita Rani and Rachel Parris play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group.(n)

programme.title: Belsen: Our Story
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the concentration camp, featuring personal accounts from the few remaining survivors and archive footage shot by the British forces that liberated them. Bergen-Belsen was used to hold prisoners evacuated from camps that had fallen to the Allied advance, leading its population to increase to nearly 60,000 by the winter of 1944. Thousands died at the camp from starvation and disease, their bodies left unburied. The British and Canadian forces who discovered the camp were left with no choice but to burn it to the ground.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani consider the profit-making potential of more business ideas. A German-Portuguese partnership proposes a new kind of camera lens for mobile phones, a lifelong baker shares her recipe for vegan treats, and a third team pitches their idea for intravenous vitamins. Evan Davis presents.(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon reaches the United States, travelling down the Rocky Mountains from Montana to Colorado. In the wide-open prairies of Montana, the cowboy way of life is under threat from a long-term decline in the rural economy, but Simon meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land. In a country with the highest incarceration rate in the world, Simon's final destination on this leg is a Colorado town that depends almost entirely on 11 prisons. Simon witnesses how inmates are put to work for the state, and how virtual reality is being used to prepare prisoners for life on the outside.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look through the BBC archive to discover what makes an epic, featuring interviews with some of those who starred in some of the biggest film in the world. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: The Vikings (1958)
programme.desc: A Norse warrior becomes embroiled in a feud with a former slave over the succession to the Northumbrian throne, unaware his arch-enemy is really his half-brother. Matters come to a head when they both set their sights on a Welsh princess, leading to a confrontation - but the situation is further complicated by the interference of England's sadistic king. Adventure, with Kirk Douglas, Tony Curtis, Janet Leigh, Ernest Borgnine and James Donald.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Five experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, with David Harper excited by an early device for watching films, while David Fletcher and James Lewis reminisce about Rupert the Bear books and Dinky toys. Paul Martin enjoys a ride in a tank on historic former military camp.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mathematician Rachel Riley, singer JB Gill, newscaster Katie Derham and comedian Richard Herring take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Excitement is mounting as the Park keepers hope that rare Amur tiger Yana may be pregnant. Meanwhile, a rare little Hyrax is in trouble.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers ride across the Balearic islands Menorca and Majorca, sampling great produce and finding out about the British influence in the Mediterranean from the 17th century to the present day. They reveal how mayonnaise was invented in Menorca's capital of Mahon, sample Menorcan gin, and meet British chef Mark Fosh, who has lived in Majorca for more than 20 years. Recipes include cuttlefish stew and squid salad with mayonnaise, fish and chips in gin and tonic batter, and sea bass a la mallorquina and saffron potato puree.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Tom Barnes, Sam Buckley, Hrishikesh Desai and George Farrugia compete in the heats for the North West. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, specifically tasked with honouring the theme of action and adventure. Presented by Susan Calman.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Lily and her boyfriend both work as programmers at the vast campus of quantum computer company Amaya, near San Francisco. When Sergei comes to the CEO's attention, he is recruited to a secretive division of the company - but when he fails to return home, an alarmed Lily begins to investigate his disappearance. Mystery thriller created by Alex Garland (Ex Machina, Annihilation), starring Sonoya Mizuno, Nick Offerman, Zach Grenier and Alison Pill.(n)

programme.title: Mister Winner
programme.desc: Jemma asks Leslie to wait in for a parcel for her while she goes to a business trip, but Leslie has other ideas and plans to go to a Dr Who convention with his best friend Cooper. Feeling guilty about letting her down, Leslie decides to cancel his day out with Cooper and stays at home to wait in for the package, which is a gift for his father-in-law. However, things do not go to plan when Leslie manages to get himself locked out of the flat, before almost burning the place down. Comedy, starring Spencer Jones and Lucy Pearman.(n)

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Comedy short, written by and starring Diane Morgan (Philomena Cunk). A woman becomes obsessed with a sofa she has seen in a shop window. Unable to think of anything else, she goes to desperate lengths to possess the furniture of her dreams.(n)

Presented by Evan Davis.(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams venture through Peru's high altitudes as the race reaches the halfway mark - but the first hurdle they face is bypassing the ongoing state of emergency in Ecuador, and drastic measures are required to ensure the competitors' safety. Emon and Jamiul are in the lead but massively over budget, so try to get work with some local fishermen, while Dom and Lizzie discover a connection to their grandmother in Peru's capital.(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary looks at Alder Hey Children's Hospital's craniofacial department - one of only four in the UK that treats life-threatening congenital skull and facial abnormalities. With a rise in referrals, the department is over-subscribed and neurosurgeon Chris Parks, who leads the craniofacial surgical team, faces a non-stop workload. Chris always works hand in glove with Ben Robertson, one of three paediatric maxillofacial surgeons in the UK. But this partnership is about to be broken up, as Ben is leaving to return to his native Australia. It takes 23 years' training to become a maxillofacial Consultant specialising in cranio-surgery, and there is currently nobody experienced enough to replace Ben, putting extra pressure on the waiting lists.(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A badly injured climber is dangling off a cliff and needs to be saved by the Mountain Rescue team. In Wales, a young boy's missing dog is found by a dog rescuer with a drone. Narrated by Kevin Duala.(n)

programme.desc: Chef Brian Turner takes guests back to their youths by visiting locations important to them and then cooking dishes inspired by their childhoods. In the first edition he is joined by cookery writer and former Great British Bake Off judge Mary Berry.(n)

programme.title: Those Magnificent Men in Their Flying Machines (1965)
programme.desc: A publisher offers to sponsor the first London-to-Paris air race in a bid to boost the sales of his newspaper. Among the motley assortment of characters who sign up for the challenge is a scheming nobleman bent on sabotaging the efforts of the other entrants. Period comedy, starring Terry-Thomas, Sarah Miles, James Fox, Robert Morley, Benny Hill, Tony Hancock, Eric Sykes and Gert Frobe.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Nick Hall assess items brought to Boconnoc House in Cornwall, while presenter Paul martin participates in a traditional Cornish gig race.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley, singer JB Gill, newscaster Katie Derham and comedian Richard Herring compete to see who can master the tasks set by Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The vets operate on Violet the koala to investigate her breathing problems, and the keepers wonder which will come off worst when a tree porcupine comes face to face with a skunk.(n)

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer digs out his rucksack and heads for Windermere in the Lake District. The National Park attracts 18 million visitors each year, and is now a World Heritage site. Here, Paul finds out why the early tourists thought the Lakes was one of the most dangerous spots in the country. He also plunges into the world of long-distance swimming and helps the staff who run the boats on the lake and meets a Holocaust survivor who was airlifted to Windermere in 1945 as part of a mercy mission involving 300 orphans after the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels from the shores of Loch Hope to the lonely expanse of Sandwood Bay in the far north-west. Along the way he discovers wartime secrets in Loch Eriboll, crosses a subterranean loch and learns the art of drystone dyking from a master craftsman in Kinlochbervie.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs from the North West cook mains and desserts celebrating children's literature, and while one takes inspiration from JM Barrie and his own imaginary feast, the other two choose Beatrix Potter, leaving judge Michael O'Hare to decide which lamb dish is better.(n)

programme.title: Devs
programme.desc: Lily's investigation leads her to the man who knows Sergei's secrets, and stirs suspicion about her employer, Forest. But when he tries to throw her off the scent, she becomes more driven to find answers, and turns again to Jamie - although he is concerned how she will act upon whatever he finds. Mystery thriller, starring Sonoya Mizuno, Nick Offerman, Zach Grenier and Alison Pill.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee hosts this episode of the comedy showcase recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, featuring stand-up performances by New York-based Edinburgh comedy award winner Alex Edelman, and at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe Comedians' Choice Award winner Lou Sanders.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Host Mary Beard gets to grips with the latest broadcast technology as she aims to present the latest series of the arts review show from her study each week. And with cultural institutions around the country closed, the programme aims to bring content alive through interviews, discussions and exclusive performances from leading writers, artists, actors and musicians. The first show features an exclusive puppet production based on a short story by Edgar Allan Poe, created in confinement in Canada by leading author Margaret Atwood and her sister Ruth.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Weird Nature
programme.desc: Creatures that go to extraordinary lengths to disguise or defend themselves, including the dressing crab, which adorns itself with everything from coral and seaweed to pearls and lace. Plus, the amazing hagfish which can turn water into slime by squirting mucus, trapping would-be predators.(n)

programme.title: Egypt's Lost Queens
programme.desc: Professor Joann Fletcher explores what it was like to be a woman of power in ancient Egypt, examining four of the civilisation's most powerful female rulers through a wealth of spectacular buildings, personal artefacts and amazing tombs. As well as the women who held the title of pharaoh, many others played key roles in running the state and shaping every aspect of life.(n)

programme.title: From Andy Pandy to Zebedee: The Golden Age of Children's TV
programme.desc: The story of the struggle to deliver television programmes for children, who were an often overlooked audience in the days before everything went digital. Includes interviews with pioneers of children's TV, such as Bernard Cribbins, Derek Griffiths, Janet Ellis, Johnny Ball and John Craven. Narrated by Nigel Planer.(n)

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: The Act of Supremacy has declared Henry the supreme head of the church in England, but Holy Roman emperor Charles V and his ambassador Eustache Chapuys refuse to recognise the new title or the monarch's divorce and subsequent marriage. With Anne pregnant and away from court, Henry begins to take more notice of Jane Seymour, whose family enlist Cromwell's help in their dealings with the king - much to Anne's displeasure. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Clare Foy and Mathieu Amalric.(n)

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne accuses Cromwell of betrayal when she finds out he tried to protect Mary and not Elizabeth at a time of crisis, but the queen's power is dissolving rapidly and her enemies are gathering. Her sister-in-law Jane Rochford reveals to Cromwell that Mark Smeaton, Francis Weston and Henry Norris have all declared their love to Anne, which is a treasonable offence, and further insinuates that her husband George Boleyn's unhealthy sexual appetite extends to his sister. Historical drama, starring Mark Rylance, Claire Foy and Jessica Raine.(n)

programme.title: Basquiat - Rage to Riches
programme.desc: Documentary, exploring the rise in profile of the output of American artist Jean-Michel Basquiat, whose work is currently the subject of a large-scale exhibition at the Barbican in London - the first time a wholly Basquiat collection has been displayed in the UK. This film serves as a portrait of Jean-Michel's origins, making use of interviews with his sisters Lisane and Jeanine, along with accounts from his friends, confidants, lovers and contemporaries, to detail how a self-taught, Brooklyn-born artist came to rank alongside the likes of Picasso, de Kooning and Francis Bacon on the international art marketplace - 29 years after his death.(n)

programme.title: From Andy Pandy to Zebedee: The Golden Age of Children's TV
programme.desc: The story of the struggle to deliver television programmes for children, who were an often overlooked audience in the days before everything went digital. Includes interviews with pioneers of children's TV, such as Bernard Cribbins, Derek Griffiths, Janet Ellis, Johnny Ball and John Craven. Narrated by Nigel Planer.(n)

programme.title: Egypt's Lost Queens
programme.desc: Professor Joann Fletcher explores what it was like to be a woman of power in ancient Egypt, examining four of the civilisation's most powerful female rulers through a wealth of spectacular buildings, personal artefacts and amazing tombs. As well as the women who held the title of pharaoh, many others played key roles in running the state and shaping every aspect of life.(n)

programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Japan
programme.desc: A look at the work of the Hamadas, one of Japan's most famous families of potters, following Tomoo as he creates and fires pots in a wood-fuelled kiln.(n)

programme.title: Six Wives with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian explores the fate of the last three wives of Henry VIII. First, Lucy witnesses Henry's doomed courtship with Anne of Cleves, with Henry successfully reneging on their marriage having claimed it had yet to be consummated. Two weeks later, Henry married teenager Catherine Howard, but an act of adultery with a courtier saw the young woman convicted of treason and beheaded. Finally, Lucy explores Henry's relationship with his last wife Katherine Parr, and how she managed to win Henry round despite their greatly differing religious beliefs.(n)

programme.title: Rebuilding Notre Dame: Inside the Great Cathedral Rescue
programme.desc: Following efforts to rebuild the world-famous Cathedral of Notre Dame de Paris, a year on from the devastating fire that ravaged the 850-year-old Gothic masterpiece. Chief architect Philippe Villeneuve urgently needs a complete picture of the damage sustained during the fire, so he initiates an unprecedented collaboration between architects and scientists. Their mission is to meticulously analyse the fallen timber, stone and fractured glass, to develop a decontamination and restoration plan. This unique opportunity offers a new insight into the medieval materials, techniques and people who built Notre Dame.(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Helen Czerski explores the science of temperature. She begins by venturing to the bottom of the scale, revealing how cold has shaped the world and why frozen does not necessarily mean what people might think.(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Dr Helen Czerski explores the narrow band of temperature that has led to life on Earth. She reveals how life began in a place where hot meets cold, and how every living creature on Earth depends on temperature for its survival. She uncovers the extraordinary natural engineering that animals have evolved to keep their bodies at the right temperature and she witnesses the remarkable surgery that is using temperature to take the human body to the extremes.(n)

programme.title: Antony Gormley: How Art Began
programme.desc: For a long time it was commonly believed that art began with the cave paintings of Ice Age Europe, tens of thousands of years ago. But now, extraordinary new discoveries around the world are overturning that idea. Here sculptor Antony Gormley travels across the globe to piece together a new story of when humans discovered their creativity. Along the way he views haunting works deep inside caves across France, Spain and Indonesia, finds images created by hunter-gatherers that surprise him with their tenderness and he meets experts who are turning back the clock to when art first began.(n)

programme.title: Leningrad and the Orchestra That Defied Hitler
programme.desc: Amanda Vickery and Tom Service tell the story of the remarkable performance at the Philharmonic Hall in Leningrad in 1942 of Shostakovich's 7th Symphony. In the midst of a siege that would ultimately kill around 750,000 civilians, an orchestra of starving musicians was assembled to perform the composer's latest work, which itself had to be smuggled into the city after being completed while Shostakovich had been evacuated. This film explores the development of the music and features extracts from the symphony, filmed in the same location as the historic concert, and conducted by Maxim Shostakovich, the composer's son.(n)

programme.title: Rebuilding Notre Dame: Inside the Great Cathedral Rescue
programme.desc: Following efforts to rebuild the world-famous Cathedral of Notre Dame de Paris, a year on from the devastating fire that ravaged the 850-year-old Gothic masterpiece. Chief architect Philippe Villeneuve urgently needs a complete picture of the damage sustained during the fire, so he initiates an unprecedented collaboration between architects and scientists. Their mission is to meticulously analyse the fallen timber, stone and fractured glass, to develop a decontamination and restoration plan. This unique opportunity offers a new insight into the medieval materials, techniques and people who built Notre Dame.(n)

programme.title: How to Make
programme.desc: Designer Zoe Laughlin sets out to redesign audio headphones, an intimate item that transport the wearer to another place. In search of inspiration she heads to Hull University to discover a new substance that can turn any surface into a speaker - from a wall to a table and even the bone of a skull. Zoe also looks at the device's history, from the stethoscope-style home contraptions of the 19th century and the game-changing 1980s Sony Walkman. But what will she come up with in her own design?(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema

programme.title: Secrets of Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jamie Bartlett investigates how technology companies are shaping the future of the world, and the potential negative consequences of the drive towards progress. He visits Hyderabad, where the utopian vision promoted by Uber has left many drivers on the brink of suicide, questions the impact of more jobs becoming automated, and meets a former executive who fears social breakdown and the collapse of capitalism.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Stratford-upon-Avon, Simon and Steve want a stylish garden that mixes modern looks with heritage touches and dramatic twists, suitable for the home town of William Shakespeare. They therefore task Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich to come up with something that's equal part both stylishly modern and with hints of Tudor heritage.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. The first arrival is Suzanne from London, who hopes that mechanical expert Steve Fletcher can bring a decorative wind-up bird cage that once belonged to her late mother back to its former glory. Meanwhile, church wardens Brian Tidswell and David Dickinson bring in a selection of carved angels for wood restorer Will Kirk to repair. Elsewhere, an old Georgian peat bucket and a surgeon's leather case are restored by experts Dominic Chinea and Suzie Fletcher.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final stage of the culinary contest begins, with the four remaining contestants embarking on a culinary adventure of a lifetime on the island of Mauritius, where they will face three extraordinary challenges. The first challenge takes place in the town of Mahebourg, one of the island's earliest established fishing ports. Split into teams of two, each pair will be tasked with cooking two traditional Mauritian street food dishes for local fishermen. Next, the amateurs travel to the island's capital, Port Louis, where they have the honour of working at the Labourdonnais hotel during a busy lunch service. Finally, the contestants face the daunting challenge of creating a dinner for mentors Chef Moroogun Coopen and Chef Peeroo Nizam, as well as some of the leading figures in Mauritius' food and cultural community.(n)

programme.desc: Keegan protests his innocence to the police but learns the evidence is stacked against him, and he even fails to get Jack on his side. Iqra is shocked to see Ash's calculating side and questions whether she really knows her girlfriend at all. Whitney tells Sonia she is convinced she is going to prison, Gray issues Michaela with a warning when she tries to see his client and Kush is torn when Tommy makes him promise to keep his secret.(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lou is forced to go to extreme lengths to obtain incriminating evidence on Lenny, even if it comes at a terrible cost. Kim worries about her first post-Bob date, and Madonna's relationship with Scarlett is put to the test during the Easter Egg hunt.(n)

programme.title: Our Girl
programme.desc: The soldiers on the rescue mission make it safely back to the hospital, only to be confronted with an unexpected situation that leaves the whole section reeling. Georgie returns to the UK, trying to conceal her trauma, but is horrified to discover that Omar has struck a deal with the US to escape justice in return for providing information. When she angrily challenges the brigadier over this, she finds herself facing a mental health evaluation that leaves her future in the army hanging in the balance.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Stratford-upon-Avon, Simon and Steve want a stylish garden that mixes modern looks with heritage touches and dramatic twists, suitable for the home town of William Shakespeare. They therefore task Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich to come up with something that's equal part both stylishly modern and with hints of Tudor heritage.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. The first arrival is Suzanne from London, who hopes that mechanical expert Steve Fletcher can bring a decorative wind-up bird cage that once belonged to her late mother back to its former glory. Meanwhile, church wardens Brian Tidswell and David Dickinson bring in a selection of carved angels for wood restorer Will Kirk to repair. Elsewhere, an old Georgian peat bucket and a surgeon's leather case are restored by experts Dominic Chinea and Suzie Fletcher.(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The final stage of the culinary contest begins, with the four remaining contestants embarking on a culinary adventure of a lifetime on the island of Mauritius, where they will face three extraordinary challenges. The first challenge takes place in the town of Mahebourg, one of the island's earliest established fishing ports. Split into teams of two, each pair will be tasked with cooking two traditional Mauritian street food dishes for local fishermen. Next, the amateurs travel to the island's capital, Port Louis, where they have the honour of working at the Labourdonnais hotel during a busy lunch service. Finally, the contestants face the daunting challenge of creating a dinner for mentors Chef Moroogun Coopen and Chef Peeroo Nizam, as well as some of the leading figures in Mauritius' food and cultural community.(n)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Gregor MacLeod looks at both sweet and savoury salty recipes. The host visits a serious foodie in Point in Lewis who gives him a masterclass on how make Calzone filled with tasty Italian meats and cheese. In Kyle of Lochalsh Gregor samples crispy cheese biscuits served with a spicy apple and cranberry chutney. Plus, Gregor demonstrates that salty doesn't necessarily have to mean savoury with his own salted caramel chocolate puddings.(n)

programme.title: An Lan - A' Tuigsinn an Lain/Tide - Understanding the Tide
programme.sub-title: An Lan - Beo leis an Lan/Tide - Living with the Tide
programme.desc: The people who depend on the tide for their living, those whose lifestyle revolves around its ebb and flow, as well as those for whom changes to tidal patterns threaten their whole way of life. A thrilling annual beach horse race is held on a date dictated by the tides on Omey, an Irish tidal island.(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes view of the airports across the Highlands and Islands, through the eyes of the people who work in them. At Sumburgh Airport in Shetland, Avril Irvine and Sandra Moore work on the check-in desk as the weather closes in, and oil workers are diverted to Sumburgh for their journey home - but one goes missing.(n)

programme.title: Tales from the Coast with Robson Green
programme.desc: The actor explores the hidden coves and remote islands of Pembrokeshire and meets up with former Soldier Soldier co-star and singing partner Jerome Flynn - who is now living on the coast. On Skokholm Island, he also spends 24 hours living and working with its only human inhabitants - wildlife wardens Giselle Eagle and Richard Brown.(n)

programme.desc: Julie Etchingham talks to the teenager at the centre of the infamous case, who reported being gang raped to the police in Cyprus, only to end up on trial for making the story up - which she and her lawyers have strenuously denied. programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster leaves central Manchester, heading east along the Rochdale Canal. Not long into his journey, John finds himself struggling to contend with 19th-century technology at a cotton works. Further up the canal, he helps out members of an conservation group clearing the waterway of dumped supermarket trollies. After passing into West Yorkshire, it's on to Hebden Bridge, famous for the hard-wearing material corduroy, before John tries his hand at clog-making in one of Britain's few remaining factories dedicated to the craft.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp: On the NHS Frontline
programme.desc: First of two programmes in which Ross Kemp follows frontline medical staff in Milton Keynes Hospital Trust as they prepare for and face the coronavirus pandemic. He witnesses the sacrifices staff and their families are making in their battle against the disease. And he follows the journey of patients admitted into the hospital's respiratory ward with mild symptoms, through to the intensive care units, where those with severe Covid-19 fight for their lives.(n)

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Summer of Love (San Francisco and Napa Valley)
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix head to San Francisco, the free-spirited capital of the United States. They start out by sampling the latest trends of using cannabis in cooking, which was legalised in California in 2018 - but as they enjoy a hippy afternoon tea, Gino overindulges and chaos ensues, especially when the next activity on the agenda is a session of goat yoga. Moving on, the trio dig for clams on the Pacific coast, take a hot-air ride to a vineyard and visit a factory in Chinatown where fortune cookies are made. It seems a competition is in order and so the travel companions see who can make the most cookies in 60 seconds.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy feels total dread when Vinny's father reveals he has come back for his son, while Leyla and Kerry worry when they realise Lydia has yet to be seen on her wedding day. Dan insists to an attentive Amelia that he can look after himself, and Vanessa and Charity are dropped off at the chemotherapy unit.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Amy's party soon gets out of hand when naked pictures of Asha are sent from Corey's phone to the â€˜party people' group chat and Corey accidentally smashes one of Tracy's new lamps. Leanne catches Steve red-handed, Johnny has an unwanted visitor and Ed and Aggie sit down to a romantic dinner.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.sub-title: You've Been Framed! Gold: Top 100 Kids
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates a selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile-phone footage from the You've Been Framed! archives featuring children, from messy eaters to troublesome teens and sibling rivalries.(n)

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Tiffany Haddish chats to Ellen about hosting the relaunch of Kids Say the Darnedest Things.(n)

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Daniel from Birmingham is given five menus constructed by a quintet of potential sweethearts, and must decide which three he would like to meet based on the meals they planned.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Michael Jackson impersonator Mitch, bar manager Aiden and student JJ try to impress Becky, a student from Leeds, with their outfit choices.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy brings her daughter to work when she finds herself without a babysitter, while Dina chases a shoplifter.(n)

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy and Jonah become competitive when he passes his employee training test with flying colours. A secret shopper visits the store. Comedy, starring America Ferrera.(n)

programme.title: Sing (2016)
programme.desc: A theatre-owning koala sets up a singing competition in a last-ditch bid to stay afloat. Animation, with the voices of Matthew McConaughey, Reese Witherspoon, Seth MacFarlane and Scarlett Johansson.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: Sing (2016)
programme.desc: A theatre-owning koala sets up a singing competition in a last-ditch bid to stay afloat. Animation, with the voices of Matthew McConaughey, Reese Witherspoon, Seth MacFarlane and Scarlett Johansson.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: The Harpers help Walden prepare a romantic meal in time for his date with Bridget, but Alan is given cause to regret his charity when the visiting billionaire accidentally persuades Jake to drop out of school. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer, Angus T Jones and Ashton Kutcher.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Trying to rebound from his recent separation, Walden goes looking for Mrs Right. Meanwhile, Alan has an opportunity to reconcile with his on-again, off-again girlfriend Lyndsey. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's estranged sister Karen arrives in Quahog for Thanksgiving and Peter's friends soon recognise her as a pro wrestler named Heavy Flo.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire tells Ida that he is in love with Lois, which Peter accidentally overhears. Peter becomes furious with his friend and Lois is forced to to resolve the situation.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Juice
programme.desc: Comedy quiz hosted by Keith Lemon, with team captains Holly Willoughby and Mel B joined by singers Anne-Marie and Fleur East, reality star Joey Essex, all-round entertainer John Barrowman and, from RuPaul's Drag Race UK, The Vivienne and Cheryl Hole.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Joe is injured when Peter, Cleveland, and Quagmire decide to run with the bulls, while Brian sleeps with a woman who is married to a Navy Seal.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona panics over keeping the salon going. Samir receives good news from the doctors about becoming a donor. Jon tells Ken that Denise has left and taken Daniel with her.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre, Samir and Tracy struggle for space in Ken's flat. Martin finds out the results of his nursing exams. Fiona calls in her friend Maxine to help out at the salon.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe wants to carry on as normal after the attack, but Emma wants her to report it. Sam gets fed up of being bossed around by Eric. Frank confronts Ken Adlington.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is worried that Frank has found out about her and Dave. Sam enlists Butch to help with Eric's house clearance. Zoe makes a statement to the police about the attack.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rachel's informant leads police to a drugs shipment at a deserted cove. The perpetrator's girlfriend, having struck up a friendship with Gina at antenatal classes, agrees to use her to help him escape - but a hostage crisis ensues, leaving the barmaid in great danger. Peggy comes up with alternative transport when Bernie worries his hearse won't be ready for a funeral. Tim Brooke-Taylor makes a guest appearance.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Dervish and Co are back at the crease but with ball tampering and rule bending rife, a fair game seems out of the question. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger worries about who will make the team's teas when Mim is taken ill. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan and Philip goad Rigsby into fasting for three days - and it is not long before he is stealing Vienna's cat food to stave off the pangs of hunger. With Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff fails to control of a violent outbreak at the bookmakers, and is in awe of Joe as he takes command of the situation. Debt collector Mick Revill (guest star Martin Kemp) suggests a way to toughen him up, but elsewhere Revill is accused by a local businessman of being involved in some dubious deals. Meanwhile, Peggy can't understand David's reluctance to attend a centenary party at the school.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Britt and Terry get packed up and ready for their move. Eric's plans for the trip to France worry Frank. A fierce argument erupts between Britt and Terry at their leaving party.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A double decker bus arrives outside the Woolpack, ready for the trip to France. Frank is arrested for assaulting Ken Adlington. The bus breaks down putting the trip in jeopardy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don and Josie plan an intimate weekend together. Tracy tells Ken she wishes Deirdre had never married Samir. Martin secretly plans a family caravanning holiday.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin and Gail are horrified to find the Duckworths in the caravan next to them. Percy, Phyllis and Betty reminisce about VE Day. Fiona worries about Denise's whereabouts.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris).(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff fails to control of a violent outbreak at the bookmakers, and is in awe of Joe as he takes command of the situation. Debt collector Mick Revill (guest star Martin Kemp) suggests a way to toughen him up, but elsewhere Revill is accused by a local businessman of being involved in some dubious deals. Meanwhile, Peggy can't understand David's reluctance to attend a centenary party at the school.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: Returning for a second attempt at the Hatton Garden safe deposit vault, the gang members, now led by Terry Perkins, set out to finish what they started. They must obtain new equipment and get back into the building, but time is getting tight with the bank holiday weekend slipping by. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Kenneth Cranham.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: With Violet gone, Sidney loses himself in drink. After a heavy whiskey session leaves him without his jacket - and little memory of the night before - he discovers to his horror that he was the last person to see a young woman alive. As he struggles to remember her final words, Geordie grows concerned - both for the murdered girl, so callously abandoned outside a local slum, and for his friend's troubled state of mind.Â James Norton stars, with Robson Green, Tessa Peake-Jones, Al Weaver and Kacey Ainsworth.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As the team see victory in their grasp, Fred has a heart attack. Cricketing drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Tongues are set wagging when a few unlikely liaisons are formed at the Brent Park barbecue. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby challenges Philip to a boxing match, convinced Miss Jones will throw herself at his feet if he wins. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An explosion in the woods is linked to the activities of a villain working out of a local quarry. Rachel suspects the owner of supplying dynamite to armed robbery gangs, so Joe goes undercover to infiltrate the workforce - but ends up in mortal danger when his cover is blown. Meanwhile, Peggy sees an opportunity to make money when rumours abound that a meteorite caused the blast. Guest starring Bill Ward and Graeme Garden.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tempers flare while the bus trippers wait to be rescued. The Dingles unsuccessfully attempt to hitch a ride home. Zoe confides in Sarah about the prejudices she's received.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty insists on a refund from Eric for the French trip. Terry confides in Alan that he is missing Britt. Zoe rows with Emma and tells her to stop controlling her life.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack spends a cold night under the Platts' caravan. Samir tells Jim he is nervous about giving his kidney to Tracy. Gail realises her relationship with Martin is worth saving.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Platts enjoy being close together. Nick and Melanie skive off school. Billy takes Betty dancing at The Ritz and reveals some stunning news from the past.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An explosion in the woods is linked to the activities of a villain working out of a local quarry. Rachel suspects the owner of supplying dynamite to armed robbery gangs, so Joe goes undercover to infiltrate the workforce - but ends up in mortal danger when his cover is blown. Meanwhile, Peggy sees an opportunity to make money when rumours abound that a meteorite caused the blast. Guest starring Bill Ward and Graeme Garden.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: When the Metropolitan Police discover the burglary after the long Easter weekend, the gang members already have a two-day lead on them. They are desperate to split up the stolen goods, but more delays frustrate them. However, they soon come to realise that it is not only the police who are chasing them - Brian Reader also wants his share.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Geordie suspects foul play when computer laboratory head Professor Simms keels over and dies from mercury poisoning. It seems the deceased recently spent the night in the lab with someone other than his wife, possibly a fellow educated man. Geordie seeks advice from Leonard, and the pair start to uncover the surprisingly messy love lives of Simms' colleagues. Elsewhere, Cathy is brutally tricked, Will returns to Grantchester and Mrs C makes a shocking discovery. Drama, starring Robson Green and Tom Brittney.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Downtrodden detective Jack Frost investigates after an unidentified man's body is found on top of a pile of glass at a council recycling centre - and the subsequent autopsy reveals a few surprises. Another case is soon on the cards when an apparent suicide raises suspicions. On the personal front, Frost decides to get in shape and joins a gym, where he falls for the charms of his fitness trainer. Starring David Jason and Claire Cox.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby discovers there is a new man in Miss Jones's life and decides to declare his feelings for her - but his romantic gesture comes too late. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar suspects fishy goings-on after veteran music manager Stan Sparrow chooses to hold his wedding reception at the Aidensfield Arms. When Stan is found dead the next day, investigations reveal his demise may not have been through natural causes. Meanwhile, Geoff is baffled by a boy reporting a crime that has yet to happen, and Peggy's conscience starts to trouble her when she hears the lad has gone missing. Guest starring Sue Jenkins.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nick is worried about Alice's first day at school. Barbara has some shocking news for Eric. Zoe arrives home to find Margaret Adlington confronting Emma.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sam Dingle is convinced that Eric is dead. Terry gets ready to visit Britt. Zoe and Emma tease Linda about Biff. Dave stands Kim up in favour of a night in with Kathy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken worries about Denise and Dan's welfare. Tracy rows with Samir when he finds her drinking. Denise phones the salon to check all is well, but has no plans to return.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf and Betty hand over their chains as their term of office ends. Ken heads to Macclesfield to find Denise. Tracy is troubled when Samir tells her he will be her donor.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar suspects fishy goings-on after veteran music manager Stan Sparrow chooses to hold his wedding reception at the Aidensfield Arms. When Stan is found dead the next day, investigations reveal his demise may not have been through natural causes. Meanwhile, Geoff is baffled by a boy reporting a crime that has yet to happen, and Peggy's conscience starts to trouble her when she hears the lad has gone missing. Guest starring Sue Jenkins.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a difficult case when he investigates the discovery of a body in a well, and he also has to contend with a new boss, Chief Superintendent Moody, who is dubious as to Lewis' motivation for coming out of retirement. As investigations into the human remains in the well proceed, an avant-garde artist's body is also discovered and at first glance it looks like a tragic drugs overdose, but Hathaway and Lewis quickly recognise the tell tale signs of murder.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: With the surveillance teams hot on their tails, the gang members squabble among themselves over who should receive a cut of the takings. Having performed such a professional raid, they are now behaving like amateurs, and with tensions running high, Perkins breaks his own golden rule by organising another meeting. Will he cut Reader in on the deal? Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham and David Hayman star in the fact-based drama.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A relaxed Will is installed as the new vicar, though is only just back at the vicarage when Geordie drops by requesting his help. Initially reluctant to be drawn in, Will is swayed when he learns that the matter concerns Adam, a profoundly deaf boy from a devout family, whose mother has recently died following a fall. Adam's father thinks his son was responsible and the truth needs uncovering to help the family heal. Will's own troubled past threatens to colour his view of the case - and Geordie realises his new friend has demons of his own. Drama, starring Robson Green and Tom Brittney.(n)

He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: Returning for a second attempt at the Hatton Garden safe deposit vault, the gang members, now led by Terry Perkins, set out to finish what they started. They must obtain new equipment and get back into the building, but time is getting tight with the bank holiday weekend slipping by. Drama, starring Timothy Spall and Kenneth Cranham.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: With Violet gone, Sidney loses himself in drink. After a heavy whiskey session leaves him without his jacket - and little memory of the night before - he discovers to his horror that he was the last person to see a young woman alive. As he struggles to remember her final words, Geordie grows concerned - both for the murdered girl, so callously abandoned outside a local slum, and for his friend's troubled state of mind.Â James Norton stars, with Robson Green, Tessa Peake-Jones, Al Weaver and Kacey Ainsworth.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack returns to duty after a period of suspension, and finds himself investigating the murder of a man who had been leading a double life. In addition, he also has to deal with the baffling case of a man found dead with his right foot hacked off and replaced by a severed left foot. Drama, starring David Jason and Amanda Root.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger joins forces with Mim to tackle a double challenge - the threat of a game being called off due to rain, and a challenge to his leadership from another member of the team. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don and Josie plan an intimate weekend together. Tracy tells Ken she wishes Deirdre had never married Samir. Martin secretly plans a family caravanning holiday.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin and Gail are horrified to find the Duckworths in the caravan next to them. Percy, Phyllis and Betty reminisce about VE Day. Fiona worries about Denise's whereabouts.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Britt and Terry get packed up and ready for their move. Eric's plans for the trip to France worry Frank. A fierce argument erupts between Britt and Terry at their leaving party.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A double decker bus arrives outside the Woolpack, ready for the trip to France. Frank is arrested for assaulting Ken Adlington. The bus breaks down putting the trip in jeopardy.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Geoff fails to control of a violent outbreak at the bookmakers, and is in awe of Joe as he takes command of the situation. Debt collector Mick Revill (guest star Martin Kemp) suggests a way to toughen him up, but elsewhere Revill is accused by a local businessman of being involved in some dubious deals. Meanwhile, Peggy can't understand David's reluctance to attend a centenary party at the school.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As the team see victory in their grasp, Fred has a heart attack. Cricketing drama, starring Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Tongues are set wagging when a few unlikely liaisons are formed at the Brent Park barbecue. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby challenges Philip to a boxing match, convinced Miss Jones will throw herself at his feet if he wins. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An explosion in the woods is linked to the activities of a villain working out of a local quarry. Rachel suspects the owner of supplying dynamite to armed robbery gangs, so Joe goes undercover to infiltrate the workforce - but ends up in mortal danger when his cover is blown. Meanwhile, Peggy sees an opportunity to make money when rumours abound that a meteorite caused the blast. Guest starring Bill Ward and Graeme Garden.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tempers flare while the bus trippers wait to be rescued. The Dingles unsuccessfully attempt to hitch a ride home. Zoe confides in Sarah about the prejudices she's received.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty insists on a refund from Eric for the French trip. Terry confides in Alan that he is missing Britt. Zoe rows with Emma and tells her to stop controlling her life.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack spends a cold night under the Platts' caravan. Samir tells Jim he is nervous about giving his kidney to Tracy. Gail realises her relationship with Martin is worth saving.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Platts enjoy being close together. Nick and Melanie skive off school. Billy takes Betty dancing at The Ritz and reveals some stunning news from the past.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An explosion in the woods is linked to the activities of a villain working out of a local quarry. Meanwhile, Peggy sees an opportunity to make money when rumours abound that a meteorite caused the blast. Guest starring Bill Ward and Graeme Garden.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: When the Metropolitan Police discover the burglary after the long Easter weekend, the gang members already have a two-day lead on them. They are desperate to split up the stolen goods, but more delays frustrate them. However, they soon come to realise that it is not only the police who are chasing them - Brian Reader also wants his share.(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Geordie suspects foul play when computer laboratory head Professor Simms keels over and dies from mercury poisoning. It seems the deceased recently spent the night in the lab with someone other than his wife, possibly a fellow educated man. Geordie seeks advice from Leonard, and the pair start to uncover the surprisingly messy love lives of Simms' colleagues. Elsewhere, Cathy is brutally tricked, Will returns to Grantchester and Mrs C makes a shocking discovery. Drama, starring Robson Green and Tom Brittney.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam.(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Downtrodden detective Jack Frost investigates after an unidentified man's body is found on top of a pile of glass at a council recycling centre - and the subsequent autopsy reveals a few surprises. Another case is soon on the cards when an apparent suicide raises suspicions. On the personal front, Frost decides to get in shape and joins a gym, where he falls for the charms of his fitness trainer. Starring David Jason and Claire Cox.(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar suspects fishy goings-on after veteran music manager Stan Sparrow chooses to hold his wedding reception at the Aidensfield Arms. When Stan is found dead the next day, investigations reveal his demise may not have been through natural causes. Meanwhile, Geoff is baffled by a boy reporting a crime that has yet to happen, and Peggy's conscience starts to trouble her when she hears the lad has gone missing. Guest starring Sue Jenkins.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nick is worried about Alice's first day at school. Barbara has some shocking news for Eric. Zoe arrives home to find Margaret Adlington confronting Emma.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sam Dingle is convinced that Eric is dead. Terry gets ready to visit Britt. Zoe and Emma tease Linda about Biff. Dave stands Kim up in favour of a night in with Kathy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken worries about Denise and Dan's welfare. Tracy rows with Samir when he finds her drinking. Denise phones the salon to check all is well, but has no plans to return.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf and Betty hand over their chains as their term of office ends. Ken heads to Macclesfield to find Denise. Tracy is troubled when Samir tells her he will be her donor.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar suspects fishy goings-on after veteran music manager Stan Sparrow chooses to hold his wedding reception at the Aidensfield Arms. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective is faced with a difficult case when he investigates the discovery of a body in a well, and he also has to contend with a new boss, Chief Superintendent Moody, who is dubious as to Lewis' motivation for coming out of retirement. As investigations into the human remains in the well proceed, an avant-garde artist's body is also discovered and at first glance it looks like a tragic drugs overdose, but Hathaway and Lewis quickly recognise the tell tale signs of murder.(n)

programme.title: Hatton Garden
programme.desc: With the surveillance teams hot on their tails, the gang members squabble among themselves over who should receive a cut of the takings. Having performed such a professional raid, they are now behaving like amateurs, and with tensions running high, Perkins breaks his own golden rule by organising another meeting. Will he cut Reader in on the deal? Timothy Spall, Kenneth Cranham and David Hayman star in the fact-based drama.(n)

programme.title: Tommy Cooper
programme.desc: Actress Diana Dors joins the incomparable comedian as he performs a variety of sketches and tricks. The ensemble cast features Peter Reeves and Clovissa Newcombe.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A coroner asks Quincy to help him find the evidence he needs to close down a substandard foster home responsible for the death of a 7 year-old boy.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A veteran officer is murdered, but is accused of corruption when he is found to be carrying $10,000. Theo has 48 hours to prove his innocence so that he can be buried with honours.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar has to undergo training to become a mercenary in an attempt to foil a hijacking. Roger Moore stars.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur witnesses the untimely death of a business acquaintance, for which Chisholm appears to hold an old friend of Terry's accountable. In the circumstances, a spell in hospital seems like a good idea - but not all of Arthur's visitors arrive with flowers and grapes. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's former girlfriend visits from East Germany and he finds himself framed for carrying out an assassination attempt - leaving Doyle with no choice but to bring him in for questioning. Drama, starring Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A Â£30,000 consignment of mercury is stolen and the squad is bemused when the prime suspect comes up with a cast-iron alibi - he was in the company of his 'mistress' hours before his planned wedding ceremony. Diana Dors guest stars in the crime drama, alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner investigates the murder of an elderly woman, but is himself assaulted and savagely beaten while searching for evidence to convict the victim's killers.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: After the owner of a jewellery company is mugged and murdered on the street, Kojak suspects that it is no ordinary crime. His investigation reveals that the man's company has recently been taken over by some very questionable people. Scatman Crothers (The Shining) guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar helps a businessman who claims he is being ruined by an imposter. Classic adventures, starring Roger Moore.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur makes his way in the glamorous world of fashion by selling on some fake designer label clothes to a buyer, only to find himself relegated to minding the frocks. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the death of a burglar who was killed after seeing classified information about a foreign country, and resort to theft in a bid to uncover the embassy's sinister secrets. Liz Fraser and Michael Elphick guest star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A Scottish detective on the trail of a gang of crooks requests help, and the squad resolves to catch his quarry. However, it becomes apparent that the visiting sleuth is holding back some important information that Regan and his colleagues need to know. TV presenter Janet Ellis features in a supporting role.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: As the winter season enters its final few weeks, the price of lobsters rises above 100 dollars per kilo, and a bunch of new boats try to capitalise on the high price. Meanwhile the Triton is hunting for big reds near Low Rocky Point, but other boats aren't the only problem - octopus are out in force, preying on rock lobster.(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Piers sits down for an interview with renowned professional footballer Cristiano Ronaldo.(n)

programme.title: Rambo III (1988)
programme.desc: Vietnam veteran John Rambo turns down a request from his old colonel to join a mission in Soviet-occupied Afghanistan. However, when the officer is captured, Rambo sets off to save him, joining the Mujahedeen rebels in fighting against the Russian invaders. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Kurtwood Smith, Marc de Jonge and Sasson Gabai.(n)

Claire has been bed-bound for the last six years. Trapped by her 40-stone body, she can only see the world through the window of the room where she spends her life. Claire has two daughters - Tracey and Jasmine - who need to do nearly everything for her. Tracey has two young children and a home of her own, so the burden of care falls mainly on 19-year-old Jasmine. programme.desc: Two more extraordinary singletons go looking for love. Twenty-one-year-old Michael, who has cerebral palsy, recently left home to study art at university in Plymouth. Can she find her very own Frank Sinatra before she reaches 30?(n)

programme.title: Welcome to the Punch (2013)
programme.desc: A criminal evades the law after a heist and goes into hiding overseas, leaving the detective on his trail tormented by his failure. He gets a second chance when the crook returns to visit his hospitalised son, but the investigation brings to light a conspiracy that threatens both men. Crime thriller, starring James McAvoy, Mark Strong, Andrea Riseborough and David Morrissey.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

Day two sees an international-themed menu being served up, while a menu of hearty cuisine and obscure meats are prepared on the final night.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

Robin and Gemma have recently returned to the UK after three years in Singapore and want a family home to bring up their baby, while in Tunbridge Wells, Gemma and Barney are looking to buy their first place together in a very competitive market.(n)

programme.desc: Choir teacher Dionne hosts the first of five parties from in and around Leeds, hoping her menu inspired by family favourites will impress Billy, Jane, Darren and Denver.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Finance director Jane hosts the second night in Leeds. Her plan to grab the grand is to treat her guests to a night of fine dining, but she has her work cut out. Drama teacher Denver is already on the back foot with Jane, after taking offence to being called a 'goth' on the first night. Car painter Darren is concerned that Jane's menu of home-made ravioli, pork and panna cotta could win the week, but he's less impressed when it gets to the table. Painter and decorator Billy has noticed a chilling change among some of the other diners and Dionne is just glad her night is out the way so she can enjoy the rest of the week.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drama teacher and DJ Denver hosts the third dinner party in Leeds, with a Spanish-inspired menu that also includes a prosecco van and stilt walkers on arrival.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Billy is concerned about the amount of mustard Darren is using in his dishes and mother-of-five Dionne causes a commotion when she offers up a hug to Jane.(n)
