programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: The actor visits York Minster, which over the past 800 years, has been a backdrop to some of the most dramatic events in British history. It houses more than half of all the world's medieval glass, including the Rose Window, commissioned by Henry VIII, and the East Window, the largest stained glass window in the world. A succession of outspoken bishops of York have defied Nazis, married kings and queens, and more recently championed equality with Archbishop John Sentamu, the country's first black bishop, consecrating the world's first female bishop, Libby Lane, in 2014.(n)

programme.desc: Documentary examining the rising number of incidents of violence and antisocial behaviour in hospitals, looking at measures implemented by Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham. programme.title: Secrets of Your Supermarket Food
programme.desc: The pros and cons of going green. Stefan Gates goes on a crash vegan diet for a month, monitoring the effect on his body. Sian Williams visits a food company that specialises in meat replacements, such as jackfruit burgers and vegan pork scratchings. There's also a comparison of vegetarian ready meals and their meat alternatives, and a look at whether it's better to buy fruit and vegetable locally or from imported from abroad.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath.(n)

programme.title: House Doctor

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: Built more than 1,400 years ago, Canterbury Cathedral is both a holy place and a World Heritage site. It is the 'mother church' for more than 85 million people, and one of the biggest tourist attractions in the UK with nearly a million visitors a year. Tony tells the tale of the cathedral's founding and how, after the cathedral burnt down, it was rebuilt by the Normans as a symbol of their power. He also explores how it became wealthy thanks to its role as a major site of pilgrimage following the infamous murder of Archbishop Thomas Becket.(n)

programme.title: Abby Hatcher
programme.desc: Princess Flug feels left out when she is the only one who does not want to play outside and get dirty.(n)

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: Diesel resents being scolded when he makes a mistake, and takes advantage of Rebecca's good nature.(n)

programme.title: Daisy & Ollie
programme.desc: The friends find out how to be well prepared in the event of a fire.(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family head to the pond to play with their toys.(n)

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: Carlos and Tracker find out Mayor Goodway and Chickaletta are in trouble.(n)

programme.title: Floogals
programme.desc: Fleeker, Flo and Boomer are puzzled by an activity book of Girl Hooman's in the playroom which has pages containing jumbled up numbers.(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Puppies
programme.desc: Young golden labrador pup Pippa and her brothers continue their training to be assistance dogs - and soon the time comes to take their test.(n)

programme.title: Milkshake! Monkey's Amazing Adventures
programme.desc: To find out more about England, Milkshake! Monkey goes on a London bus tour and tries some traditional food.(n)

programme.title: Little Princess
programme.desc: A lack of rain means that the Admiral's pond is almost empty, so Professor and Princess set out to try and find some rain clouds.(n)

programme.title: Ben and Holly's Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Gaston is sent to pet school, where Miss Jolly soon gets him into shape.(n)

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies
programme.desc: The Sunny Bunnies are planting a field of carrots when a UFO lands on planet Earth and keeps stealing their crop.(n)

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: The broadcaster and guests discuss the issues of the day, with co-host Storm Huntley joining him for phone-ins and reading out viewers' correspondence.(n)

programme.title: Tormenting Abigail (2017)
programme.desc: After leading a jury to wrongly sentence a man, a woman enters a new teaching job at a law school with a new-found commitment to ethics. However, her path soon crosses with a student who claims he will stop at nothing to get what he wants. Thriller, starring Alicia Leigh Willis and Blake Michael.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross encourages Chandler and Monica to move in together and makes a dramatic decision about Rachel. David Schwimmer, Matthew Perry, Courteney Cox and Jennifer Aniston star.(n)

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team investigates a series of escalating threats against congresswoman Jenna Flemming, while Director Vance oversees temporary protection detail. Mary Stuart Masterson guest stars.(n)

programme.title: A Daughter's Deception (2019)
programme.desc: Forced to give up her daughter for adoption when she's underage, Laura is thrilled to welcome her now adult daughter Bree back into her life, but soon discovers Bree is a sociopath. Drama, starring Jade Harlow and Skyler Wright.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One on the Last Night
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler celebrate their last night as flatmates, and Ross claims he is too busy babysitting to help Monica as she frantically tries to arrange Rachel's packing before the removal firm arrives. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry.(n)

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An Australian dancer (guest star Elle Macpherson) moves in with Joey and he struggles to refrain from flirting. Chandler tries to impress Monica by cleaning their flat, and Rachel is embarrassed by Phoebe's jogging style. Comedy, with Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry.(n)

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Documentary following life at Julian Norton and Peter Wright's veterinary practice as they and their team care for animals in the rural communities of North Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Crafting
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rosie Wolfenden follow as three hobby crafters present their works for the first time at Brancepeth Castle Craft Fair in Durham. Occupational therapist Olivia dyes her own yarn and produces hand-woven pieces, ex-builder Neil makes quirky metal ornaments and Wendi now makes her own glass pieces uses the art of fusing - and for the fair she even learns the art of glass blowing.(n)

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the secret of the supermarket chain's success. The programme examines the launch of a brand of organic food from Prince Charles' own duchy and reveals how the opening of a new Waitrose store has been known to boost house prices in the surrounding area. A taste test is performed to see how Waitrose's own brands compare to their rivals.(n)

programme.title: Springtime on the Farm
programme.desc: The final programme celebrating the hard work of the great British farmer. Helen Skelton and Adam Henson present reports about Cumbrian cows and a goat playground, and JB Gill visits a shepherdess who demonstrates how she trains sheepdogs.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Royals on Tour
programme.desc: First of two documentaries going behind the scenes with the royal family on their various travels. Through a combination of archive footage and interviews with insiders, the films reveal the significant historic occasions, most surprising secrets and incredible moments when the royals went globetrotting.(n)

programme.title: Millionaire Age Gap Love
programme.desc: Part two of two. The stories of multimillionaire celebrity hair stylist Nicky Clarke, who unexpectedly found love with his PA Kelly Simpkin, and 72-year-old millionaire Lyn, who is accustomed to dating men 40 years her junior and is on the hunting for Mr Right.(n)

programme.title: Pretty Woman (1990)
programme.desc: A ruthless millionaire hires a prostitute while on a business trip. Realising she may be useful in helping him keep up a successful appearance, he pays her to accompany him for a week as he associates with the rich and powerful - but falls in love despite their many differences. Romantic comedy, starring Richard Gere and Julia Roberts, with Ralph Bellamy and Laura San Giacomo.(n)

programme.title: The Perfect Catch (2005)
programme.desc: Unlikely couple Ben and Lindsey meet and fall in love over the winter. When summer brings a new baseball season, Lindsey discovers Ben's devotion to the Boston Red Sox baseball team knows no bounds - and suspects she will always come second. The Farrelly brothers' romantic comedy, starring Jimmy Fallon and Drew Barrymore, inspired by Nick Hornby's football-themed novel Fever Pitch, but with America's favourite sport replacing Britain's.(n)

programme.title: The Indian in the Cupboard (1995)
programme.desc: A nine-year-old boy is given an old cupboard and a toy Iroquois Indian for his birthday - but when he puts the figure inside the cupboard, he is astonished to see it come to life. He repeats the experiment with more of his toys, and realises he is creating real people - and is responsible for their wellbeing. Children's fantasy, with Hal Scardino, Litefoot and Steve Coogan.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age: The Meltdown (2006)
programme.desc: Sid the sloth, Manny the mammoth and Diego the sabre-toothed tiger grimly realise the good life is about to come to an abrupt end as flood waters threaten their homes. However, a vulture suggests they could turn an old giant tree into a boat and make their escape, so the three friends set off in search of it. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Ray Romano, John Leguizamo and Denis Leary.(n)

programme.title: Harry and the Hendersons (1987)
programme.desc: A hapless family's camping holiday in the mountains is brought to a sudden end when they accidentally hit the legendary Bigfoot with their car. They decide to adopt the endearing creature, but have more trouble than expected trying to keep him concealed and to avoid arousing their neighbours' suspicions. Comedy, starring John Lithgow, Melinda Dillon, David Suchet, Don Ameche and Kevin Peter Hall.(n)

programme.title: Home Again (2017)
programme.desc: Five months after separating from her husband, Alice Kinney and her daughters relocate from New York to her childhood home in Los Angeles, which belonged to her late father, Oscar-winning film director John Kinney. Eventually things take an unexpected turn when she lets three young guys move in with her. Romantic comedy, starring Reese Witherspoon, Michael Sheen and Candice Bergen.(n)

programme.title: The Ritual (2017)
programme.desc: A group of college friends reunite for a trip to the forest, but encounter a menacing presence in the woods that is stalking them. Horror, starring Rafe Spall.(n)

programme.title: Trainspotting (1996)
programme.desc: A heroin addict makes a series of attempts to kick the habit, but the grind of everyday life and a circle of similarly self-destructive friends keep dragging him back to his old ways. Following a near-fatal overdose, he comes to the realisation that the only way to put his life in order is to rid himself of his friends - which is easier said than done. Danny Boyle's drama based on Irvine Welsh's novel, starring Ewan McGregor, Robert Carlyle and Ewen Bremner.(n)

programme.title: American Hustle (2013)
programme.desc: A conman is caught in the act by the FBI, but a maverick agent offers him the chance to escape prison by assisting in a sting operation. The hustler finds himself involved in a scheme to bring down the corrupt, Mafia-affiliated politician, only to find his own ex-wife plotting revenge. Crime drama set in the 1970s, starring Christian Bale, Amy Adams, Bradley Cooper and Jennifer Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Rugrats in Paris: The Movie (2000)
programme.desc: The lucky little tots embark on a trip to a French theme park - but while the rest of the gang are intent on enjoying themselves, Chuckie is distracted by his wish for a new mum. He sets his sights on the owner of the amusement park as a prospective partner for his single dad. Animated adventure, with the voices of Christine Cavanaugh, Elizabeth Daily, Cheryl Chase, Kath Soucie and Susan Sarandon.(n)

programme.title: Hotel for Dogs (2009)
programme.desc: An orphaned brother and sister face losing their beloved dog when they go into a foster home that does not allow pets. The kids find the ideal hiding place in an abandoned hotel, and set about transforming it into luxury canine accommodation for not just their beloved pet, but every stray in town. Family comedy, starring Emma Roberts, Jake T Austin, Don Cheadle and Lisa Kudrow.(n)

programme.title: Short Circuit (1986)
programme.desc: A military robot is struck by lightning and develops a mind of its own. The machine embarks on a series of comic misadventures, but the company that made him wants him back. He realises his only hope is to convince the scientist who invented him that he really is alive. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Steve Guttenberg, Ally Sheedy and Fisher Stevens.(n)

programme.title: Edward Scissorhands (1990)
programme.desc: A reclusive genius creates a boy but dies before completing him, leaving his brainchild with scissor-like blades in place of hands. A kindly woman from a nearby suburb takes pity on the lonely figure and welcomes him into her home. He quickly becomes a hit in the neighbourhood - but then his new friends start to turn against him. Tim Burton's fantasy, starring Johnny Depp, Winona Ryder, Dianne Wiest and Vincent Price.(n)

programme.title: Knight and Day (2010)
programme.desc: A woman has a chance encounter with a rogue secret agent, who has been framed as a traitor and is on the run from his own superiors. Realising she has been mistaken for his accomplice and is in danger for knowing too much, the fugitive kidnaps her and drags her on a dangerous journey around the world, as he sets out to clear his name and protect a vitally important invention. Comedy adventure, with Tom Cruise, Cameron Diaz and Viola Davis.(n)

programme.title: Brassed Off (1996)
programme.desc: The leader of a South Yorkshire colliery band is determined to see its members compete in a prestigious competition, despite his own failing health - but the musicians are more concerned with the threatened closure of their pit. Drama, starring Pete Postlethwaite, Ewan McGregor, Stephen Tompkinson and Tara FitzGerald.(n)

programme.title: The Cabin in the Woods (2012)
programme.desc: Five students staying in an isolated house deep in a forest discover an ancient book that summons up supernatural creatures. As they defend themselves from the forces they have unleashed, they discover something far stranger and far more dangerous is going on behind the scenes. Horror, starring Kristen Connolly and Chris Hemsworth.(n)

programme.title: Prevenge (2016)
programme.desc: Following a terrible tragedy, single mum-to-be Ruth seems determined to wreak revenge on anyone who dares to undermine her or define her by her pregnancy. Egged on by the voice of her unborn baby, Ruth embarks on a series of brutal murders that appear to be unrelated, but her targets are far from spontaneously chosen. Black comedy, written, directed by and starring Alice Lowe, with Gemma Whelan, Kate Dickie and Tom Davis.(n)

programme.title: The SpongeBob Movie: Sponge Out of Water (2015)
programme.desc: The cheery sea creature tries to prevent a precious secret formula from falling into the wrong hands - but when it goes missing, the end of the world approaches. SpongeBob and his friends embark on a quest to get it back, which leads to them developing superpowers and venturing onto dry land. Animated comedy, with the voices of Tom Kenny and Antonio Banderas.(n)

programme.title: Mary and the Witch's Flower (2017)
programme.desc: A strange flower grants a girl magic powers. Animated adventure based on The Little Broomstick by Mary Stewart, featuring the voices of Kate Winslet and Jim Broadbent. Dubbed in English.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Nurse (1959)
programme.desc: The patients on the men's ward of Haven Hospital cause more mayhem for the staff than the rest of the hospital combined, forcing the management to send in a formidable matron to control the situation. Feeling aggrieved by her no-nonsense methods, the chaps decide the time is right to get their own back. Comedy, with Kenneth Williams, Hattie Jacques, Joan Sims, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey, Shirley Eaton and Leslie Phillips.(n)

programme.title: Young Sherlock Holmes (1985)
programme.desc: The teenage detective and his future sidekick Watson meet for the first time at boarding school. The death of Holmes's mentor sets the youngsters on their first case, and they discover that former members of an archaeological survey team have died under suspicious circumstances - pointing to a sinister Egyptian cult using hallucinogenic poisons to kill its victims. Adventure, starring Nicholas Rowe, Alan Cox and Sophie Ward.(n)

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle (2016)
programme.desc: Biopic of British sporting underdog Eddie Edwards. Determined to fulfil his dreams of becoming an Olympic athlete, Edwards trains as a ski jumper - a field in which Britain had no other competitors. He secures a place in the 1988 Winter Olympics, where his can-do attitude makes him a media sensation in the face of a losing streak. Starring Taron Egerton, Hugh Jackman and Christopher Walken.(n)

programme.title: Independence Day: Resurgence (2016)
programme.desc: Two decades have passed since Earth was saved from an extraterrestrial invasion, and in the years since, the aliens' salvaged technology has been used to transform the world. However, it may not be enough to save the human race when a second, even deadlier attack begins. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jeff Goldblum, Liam Hemsworth and Bill Pullman.(n)

programme.title: The Witch (2015)
programme.desc: An English farmer is driven from his land in 17th-century America, and settles on the edge of a sinister forest. The family suffers a series of inexplicable disasters, and as tension rises between them, the eldest daughter finds herself accused by her own parents and siblings of being a witch. Period horror, starring Anya Taylor-Joy and Ralph Ineson.(n)

programme.desc: Mel finds out that Joe's ex-wife, Tiffany, has been keeping a big secret from him after the former lovers rekindle a romantic spark. Elsewhere, Lennox works on winning a popularity contest at school. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.desc: Mel's personal trainer immediately hits it off with Joe, while Lennox struggles with a crush of her own when she tries to stay just friends with Beckett. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.desc: A prestigious writer's workshop in Iowa offers Molly a place, but as the house now includes Peggy and Carl, she struggles to find a way to break the news to Mike. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: In the fifth season premiere, Molly returns home after eight weeks at the prestigious writers' workshop with exciting news - she is getting her book published and has received a generous advance. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is stunned when Sheldon announces he is about to go on his first-ever date, and is even more surprised to find herself tagging along. Meanwhile, Leonard and Raj take a trip to the hospital after Howard's new robotic arm creates problems.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to extend his lifespan, concerned he will not be around long enough for his consciousness to be downloaded into a robot body, and the friends encounter Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak (guest starring as himself) at the Cheesecake Factory.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, focusing on an 11-year-old boy as he captures his family on video camera during the 1980s. programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: A day at the roller-skating rink leaves Murray wishing he had not asked Erica to tell him about her life, while Pops steps in to help when a back-to-school shopping trip causes problems between Beverly and Adam. Comedy, starring Jeff Garlin, Wendi McLendon-Covey and Hayley Orrantia.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Part one of two. A prison van crash sees a mass escape of convicts take place and the entire squad has to track the villains down. Peralta and Santiago make a bet on which of them can bring in the most escaped prisoners, with the loser forced to move into the winner's apartment when they start living together. Former American football star Marshawn Lynch guests.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Part two of two. Only one fugitive remains on the loose on the streets of Brooklyn after the mass escape of convicts from the prison van and Peralta is forced to enlist a surprising ally in order to apprehend him. Elsewhere, Santiago and Gina attempt to introduce Boyle to proper texting etiquette. Craig Robinson guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is forced to teach a course at the university, and Howard surprises the gang when he decides to enrol on it. Meanwhile, Amy plays Penny and Bernadette off against each other while the tension brews over Penny's new job. Comedy, guest starring Regina King (Southland), with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny attempt to prove Sheldon wrong when he claims that he and Amy have a superior relationship. Meanwhile, Howard is nervous when Nasa asks him to throw the first pitch at a baseball game honouring National Space Day. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel contemplates the future of her relationship as George is offered an amazing new job abroad, while Ryder's relationship is also becoming more serious as he meets Holly's parents. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel's father returns to the town to lobby for a bill that his daughter is strongly opposed to. Elsewhere, some of Lennox's poems catch the attention of a popular classmate who wants to become a singer. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Joyce pulls up when Carl and Mike are working a drunk-driving checkpoint, and while she throws insults at the two men, Mike is forced to take her into the station. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: The duo are excited to finally have the house to themselves for the whole weekend. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly and Murray fall out over Barry's plan to perform his martial arts routine at the annual high school talent show. Pops buys Erica an electric guitar, urging her to take to the stage and sing. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly complains that Pops is spending too much time with her at home, so Murray takes him to work at the furniture shop. Meanwhile, Adam reluctantly gives away his old toys in an effort to make him look more grown up, hoping it will result in a kiss with Dana. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Diaz learn there's more to Lt Melanie Hawkins' team than meets the eye as they continue their search for the gang of bank robbers. Jeffords, Boyle and Santiago begin to suspect that Gina is harbouring a secret. Ryan Phillippe, Gina Gershon and Jason Mantzoukas guest star.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Peralta and Diaz are framed for a crime they didn't commit, it's up to the other members of the Nine-Nine to find out what really happened and try to save them from going to jail.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj endures an anxious day as he worries that a space probe he helped design will not function properly, while Leonard and Sheldon agree to go clothes shopping with Penny and Amy. Comedy, starring Kunal Nayyar, Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Don film a commercial for their company, and Andi works up the courage to ask her boss for a raise.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jeffords believes the Pontiac Bandit is up to his old tricks and stealing cars again, but Peralta is convinced he's wrong and attempts to prove it. The rest of the squad battles to keep Shaw's bar a police hangout after some firefighters stake their claim to it.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Competitive juices are flowing as Jake and the squad compete in the second Jimmy Jab Games - a series of eccentric races and contests held in the precinct building.(n)

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: With his parents recently divorced, privately educated Will has to move to a new area and make friends at the local comprehensive, where he meets Simon, Jay and Neil. First episode of the comedy, starring Simon Bird, Joe Thomas, James Buckley and Blake Harrison.(n)

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: The four lads decide to play truant from sixth form and buy alcohol for an all-day drinking session, which is interrupted when Neil's father comes home early. Meanwhile, Simon decides to declare his love for Carli through graffiti, and the friends face the wrath of their parents.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien turn an old, unloved filing cabinet into a valuable and desirable storage unit and convert a Bakelite voltmeter into a retro Bluetooth speaker.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie enlists close family friend Gerry and mother Isobel to get her B&B room finished in time, and Dick offers his advice to a London couple constructing a water feature.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Shannon Slater-Dent and Trevor Tredgett's Bove Town House in Glastonbury, but a poor night's sleep bothers Kathryn and Trevor from Treberthan B&B.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Treberthan B&B in Par, Cornwall, where owner Kathryn Langley and her best friend Trevor Burnell set out to give their guests a taste of Cornish hospitality.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at SugarBeat Eating House in Swainsthorpe, Norfolk, run by Stevie King and David Lovett, but the establishment's cleanliness quickly comes under fire.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Fritton Arms in Norfolk, run by Ben Davenport and partner Sian Dewaal. David and Stevie are upset about their feedback so they conduct a forensic-style room inspection - complete with rubber gloves - and a paddleboat race on a nearby lake brings out everyone's competitive nature. Ben impresses the guests by revealing the hotel's aristocratic connections - but soon comes under fire for allowing pets to stay.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners reunite to find out what they have been paid, with Shannon and Trevor from Bove Town House wanting to discuss Kathryn's comments about her poor night's sleep.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A French renovation project hits crisis point and a family moves to the Costa del Sol to open their dream bar on a very limited budget. Plus, how will Spain's latest hoteliers deal with the feedback from their first guests?(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend lead a team of experts as they attempt to renovate a US army Jeep that survived the Second World War but was later damaged in an arson attack. They hope a rapid turnaround on the vehicle will dazzle and surprise its current owner, Dick Masters.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie sets out to restore the chateau's greenhouse, which has been damaged in a storm. Lee and Belinda get a 60th birthday booking so race to get their garden venue ready.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows plumber Jon Martin and ceramicist Noreen Jaafar as they transform a neglected half-acre site in Gloucestershire. However, they are unable to cut down the 27 protected trees on the plot, so their solution is to build a big, modern treehouse with stilts screwed into the earth and balconies that will have views for miles.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton returns to rural Devon to catch up a woman who transformed her old studio into a brand-new energy efficient home and workspace.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison follows the progress of a petite 40-square metre pod built on a sloping site facing the Southern Ocean.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Simon is concerned 40-year-old doorman Alfred, who was stabbed multiple times outside the social club where he works, may have internal injuries. Eighteen-year-old student George is brought into resus with blood coming from his ear and behaving erratically, following a fight at a party, and is sent for an urgent scan when consultant Phil becomes worried he may have a life-threatening bleed on his brain.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock is joined by Andrew Flintoff to battle guest captain Bill Bailey and team-mate Roisin Conaty as Jimmy Carr hosts another edition of the game show mash-up. Susie Dent is ably assisted by Rob Beckett in the Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley deals with the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton returns to rural Devon to catch up a woman who transformed her old studio into a brand-new energy efficient home and workspace.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of favourite investigations, with Jimmy Doherty heading to South Africa on the trail of sweet piquante peppers and visits the company that has exclusive rights to grow, process and market these colourful chillies. Kate Quilton is in Hawaii to investigate the potentially dangerous levels of mercury in tuna, and Matt Tebbutt finds out why the bags of ice cubes in supermarkets are so crystal clear.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp competes in the Something New from Something Old competition at the Royal Wales Show, creating vintage paper jewellery, but finds herself up against stiff competition.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ian Tan and his partner Christine Walsh hope to buy a pad near Sarasota in Florida. With a flexible budget of Â£150,000 to Â£250,000, the pair peruse a selection of options along the Gulf Coast. Presented by Jonnie Irwin.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin advises Jacky and Peter Kennett how far their Â£150,000 budget will stretch in the Tampa Bay area of Florida in the United States. Having visited the area for many a holiday, they have their eyes on an attractive home in which to spend the winter.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more gems hidden among unwanted possessions, trying to make a profit from church organ pipes and old leather sports car seats.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole calls in a locksmith to bring a safe back from the scrapheap, and a ship's wheel is left in splinters when the presenter falls out with restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie sets out to restore the chateau's greenhouse, which has been damaged in a storm. Lee and Belinda get a 60th birthday booking so race to get their garden venue ready.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by Ken Ward and his wife Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Just hours after moving in to their new Spanish home, a family must battle to avoid eviction. In France, a couple look for ways to fund the construction of their home and business, and the owners of a Spanish bar take on a mission to climb the review rankings.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw lead a team of experts who secretly restore classic cars for their unsuspecting owners. They continue by helping widower Jeremy, who was unable to work after a car accident, by giving his Fiat Spider the makeover it deserves.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie has a month to convert her old stable into a studio for the launch of her art weekends, but when she doesn't see eye to eye with mother Isobel about the design, renovations could take longer than anticipated. Belinda's promised a heated outdoor swimming pool on the chateau's website, so Lee starts constructing a solar panel system. Dick saves cash by getting creative on a new lighting project for Angel's treasury.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows Matt and Sophie White from Sussex as they build a giant family house of fun for themselves and their children. Going against the norm of designing a serious and practical home, the couple's property features a revolving bookcase door, secret dens, hiding places and a fireman's pole. Matt uses a steel frame system to allow them to change the layout of the property, and other changes include amending the window shapes and sizes, and adding a half floor to create a James Bond room.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Prunella Scales and Timothy West embark on a final canal journey in the first of two programmes exploring Vietnam and Cambodia. The trip begins in the limestone islands of Halong Bay, before travelling along the Red River to Hanoi and the ancient citadel of Hoa Lu. They also attend the famous lantern festival in the trading port of Hoi An.(n)

programme.title: Scotland's Scenic Railways
programme.desc: Beneath vast mountains and across open moorlands, Scotland's railways connect coastal towns and rural villages through a Highland landscape voted the world's most beautiful railway. This programme meets the people who work on the railway and the steam enthusiasts who safeguard these iconic routes, offering a glimpse into a diverse community united by a passion for the nation's railways.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The emergency room documentary continues, highlighting the stories of people facing adversity head-on. Erin, a 61-year-old former cancer patient with an ear infection, is rushed to St George's Hospital after she collapsed at home. Doctors fear the condition may have spread to her brain, and send her for a CT scan to find out more. Tony, a 70-year-old part-time DJ, arrives in A&E after being involved in a high-speed bicycle accident while training for a 100-mile charity bike ride, and Charlie, a 39-year-old cabin crew worker, turns up at the hospital complaining of unbearable pain after discharging himself during a previous visit, and removing the cast fitted to support his broken arm.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May put three supercars through their paces in Italy. Their weapons of choice are the 691bhp Lamborghini Aventador, McLaren's hi-tech MP4-12C, and a British brute in the form of the Noble M600. The journey begins at Nardo, a high-speed testing ground so vast it can be seen from space, and includes a nerve-racking drive in Rome before reaching its conclusion with a timed challenge against the Stig at the Imola circuit.(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: The amateur race arrives into Valkenburg aan de Geul in the Netherlands, traditionally the finish of the Amstel Gold Classic cycling race.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets off on a high-altitude trek from Kathmandu, capital of Nepal, to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, a secretive kingdom perched high on the Tibetan plateau, on the far side of the Himalayas. On a five-day expedition through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet, Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris battle huge river crossings, vast mudslides and dizzying mountain passes on a trip that would test even the hardiest new 4x4s to the limit. But Paddy, Freddie and Chris aren't crossing the Himalayas in new 4x4s. They're crossing the Himalayas in a small old Peugeot, a very compact old Renault, and Nepal's first (and only) home-built car.(n)

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: Coverage of the international event in London, as amateur drivers put their home-made, gravity-powered vehicles through an obstacle-littered course.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines the social significance of cars in the 20th century. He begins by travelling to Germany, Italy and Russia to examine how dictators kick-started the mobilization of the masses, finding tales of design brilliance, abject failure, war, fraud and double dealing. James also learns about how the British motor industry blew a gift-wrapped chance to rule the world and he gets his own back with a stunt that means bad news for one of the planet's most hated cars.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James concludes his examination of the social significance of cars by exploring how aspiration and new wealth were behind the development of some of the greatest models ever made. He tries to make sense of the baffling world of company car hierarchy by holding a travelling salesman race-off and indulges his 1980s urges with the twin delights of a Lamborghini and a Porsche. He also unveils his choice of the ultimate people's car - a vehicle that can lay claim to being the greatest in history.(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake journeys on some of the most perilous routes in the world. In Alaska, Sue Perkins and Charley Boorman drive along the notorious Dalton Highway, a dirt track originally constructed in the 1970s as a supply road for the Trans-Alaska oil pipeline. The pair struggle to cope with the Arctic conditions and constant threat of avalanches, but receive help from experienced truckers who teach them some unwritten rules for surviving the road's many hazards.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Rhod Gilbert and Greg Davies negotiate a variety of unusual hazards as they travel through Nepal, a country with one of the world's highest rates of road traffic accidents. Frequent floods and landslides mean car journeys are often fraught with danger, and matters are not helped by the varied states of Nepalese roads, which range from urban highways to crumbling mountain passes.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Hugh Dennis undertake a perilous journey through the Peruvian Andes. Their route takes in 2,000ft drops, one-lane dirt roads, remote mountain trails and muddy, humid jungle terrain, as they search for the lost city of Constitucion, once destined to be the capital of Peru.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May head to Norway to find out how a selection of cars fare when taking part in Winter Olympic events. Their experiments include firing a rocket-powered Mini off a ski jump, tackling the biathlon with 4x4s, staging a game of motorised ice hockey and timing the Jaguar XK in a speed-skating race.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captain David Mitchell is joined by TV presenter Gabby Logan and comedian Andy Hamilton, while his counterpart Lee Mack welcomes Dr Christian Jessen and EastEnder Diane Parish. Host Rob Brydon oversees proceedings on the comedy panel show as the contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves.(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Bob Mortimer and actress Patsy Kensit join team captain Lee Mack, while comedy actor Greg Davies and Pointless co-host Richard Osman are enlisted by his counterpart David Mitchell. Rob Brydon oversees proceedings as the two sides engage in a battle of wits to determine which of them can most successfully stretch the truth about their own lives.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts another round of the peculiar panel quiz and finds out how much Julia Zemiro, Tim Vine, Rob Brydon and regular panellist Alan Davies know about the theme of jolly, awarding points for the most interesting answers.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended edition of the panel show, and invites Jason Manford, Sara Pascoe, Jeremy Clarkson and Alan Davies to answer questions on the subject of 'noble rot'. Since the questions are impossible to answer, points are awarded for being interesting rather than correct.(n)

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Lister celebrates Father's Day, while Rimmer and Kryten install a new computer - the beautiful, but lethally logical Pree - that quickly puts the ship in danger. Comedy series, starring Craig Charles, Chris Barrie and Robert Llewellyn.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks to Dave Gorman, Al Murray, Rob Beckett, Sara Pascoe and Paul Chowdhry, including a challenge involving an egg cup.(n)

programme.title: Comedy Against Living Miserably
programme.desc: Stand-up comedy special, hosted and headlined by Joel Dommett, with support from Kiri Pritchard-Mclean, John Robins and Judi Love.(n)

programme.title: Jackie Stewart the Flying Scot
programme.desc: Profile of the legendary racing driver, tracing his early career and influence on Formula One.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: The crimes of Henri Landru, who targeted lonely and vulnerable women during the First World War. He seduced them with promises of marriage and lured them to houses outside Paris where the women vanished. With the police uninterested in investigating the disappearances, two women took it upon themselves to pursue Landru.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The bravery of the 333rd Field Artillery Battalion, a depleted unit of African-American soldiers who risked everything to fight off the Nazis at Bastogne, Belgium.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlain's declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Concrete channels hidden in a mountaintop forest, a collapsing bridge, a marooned military base and the upright rusting carcass of ship.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how fleas are able to jump great distances for their size and attempts to discover how fast a cheetah can run.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores animals that have developed tool-making skills to help them survive, including orangutans and crows.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Broadcaster Chris Tarrant continues his travels along the world's most challenging railways. Here he attempts to cross the Andes, setting off from the coast of Chile and heading for the Bolivian capital of Sucre. Along the way he traverses some of the driest and most inhospitable environments on the planet, hitches rides on unusual trains and deals with altitude sickness at more than 3,000 metres.(n)

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the wildlife eking out an existence in and around the scorching wilderness of the Sahara, where only the toughest survive. It's a constant struggle against dehydration, as zebra battle over dwindling rivers, camels search for water and tiny barn swallows navigate thousands of square miles of barren sand to find a life-saving oasis. The naked mole rat avoids the heat by living a subterranean existence, while the silver ant is able to take on the midday sun - but needs a 'spacesuit' to do it.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Shanghai Tower, which at a height of 632 metres is the second tallest building in the world, With a weight of 850,000 tons, it has been built to withstand winds of typhoon proportions.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of Major General Robert Laycock who rallied his ill-equipped troops to see off a German attack on Crete, Greece.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis' strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Forgotten relics from the defence of America's homeland, concealed by the banks of the Mississippi, and a concrete behemoth on the French coast that proved invulnerable to attacks.(n)

programme.title: History Hunters
programme.desc: The team acquire a personalised Victorian pistol and try to track down the original owner. A customer brings in brasses that his dad brought back from the Somme.(n)

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Nurse Gladys Emmanuel's washing-machine breaks down, and Arkwright decides to try and lure her into his home with the offer of his vintage machine.(n)

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville struggles to find some privacy when the milkwoman makes a pass at him. Nurse Gladys Emmanuel steps in by persuading Arkwright to take her to a hotel.(n)

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.sub-title: Fig Biscuits and Inspirational Toilet Rolls
programme.desc: Arkwright decides to open the shop before dawn to catch the morning rush and tries a special promotion to get rid of some of the goods, including fig biscuits and toilet rolls.(n)

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Crafty con Fletcher helps new inmate Godber settle in by giving him a few lessons on coping with prison life. First episode of the classic comedy, originally broadcast in 1974, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Betting fever sweeps Slade Prison, but the stakes rise too high for some of the inmates, stretching honour among thieves to the limit. Vintage comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale.(n)

programme.title: Porridge

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How, in 1940, the French Army suffered a heavy defeat after underestimating the Nazis' strength while relying on First World War strategies and equipment to defend its frontiers. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The history of pioneering modern-day mega structures, revealing that behind every seemingly impossible marvel of modern engineering is a cast of historic trailblazing engineers who designed new building techniques, took risks on untested materials and revolutionised their field. The first programme focuses on the aircraft carrier.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre examines how continued success for the unit could only be achieved with ever more dangerous and ambitious missions, while David Stirling's new second-in-command Captain Paddy Mayne was an officer as unpredictable and dangerous as the phase of war that was about to begin. The historian also reveals how many SAS members were captured by the Germans - and hears the extraordinary story of two of them who outran their pursuers by surviving for three days in the desert without food or fresh water.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Herbert Rowse Armstrong, who was convicted of murdering his wife with arsenic. Much of the evidence against him was circumstantial and the scientific testimony remains disputed. This episode examines both sides of the case and asks whether the British justice system made a terrible mistake.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of Major General Robert Laycock who rallied his ill-equipped troops to see off a German attack on Crete, Greece.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Chris Tarrant on the Polar Express
programme.desc: Chris sets out to reach the northernmost railway station on Earth, inside Russia's Arctic Circle. This is an epic 2,000-mile adventure from Moscow into Arctic Siberia, and begins with a marathon 19-hour leg. Along the way he enjoys a reindeer stew with a local family and takes the controls of one of the massive locomotives that power the trains, before pressing on along railways built under the brutal regime of Joseph Stalin.(n)

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.sub-title: To the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough travels to the Arctic and Antarctic to explore the wildernesses at Earth's poles, investigating the wildlife and geographical features found there. He begins by travelling to the North Pole, where two polar bears reveal a surprisingly tender side to their characters, before heading to the South Pole - 100 years after Roald Amundsen and Robert Falcon Scott first reached it.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of plants and animals that live in jungles, one of the hardest environments to thrive in, including strange fungi and creatures that glow in the dark which have never been captured on film before. The programme also includes recordings of caimans and the jaguars that prey on them, freshwater dolphins that swim through flooded forests, undergrowth-dwelling frogs that have to defend themselves against wasps, and the Indri, the world's largest living lemur, which inhabits the forests of Madagascar.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlin's Tempelhof Airport and London's Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: How Field Marshall Erich von Manstein's escape from the Red Army influenced Nato's defence plans.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An American prison for the violent and deranged with links to an infamous cult leader, and a cavernous tunnel network in France offering shelter from unrelenting bombardment.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Falkirk wheel is the first and only rotating boat lift, capable of moving 600 tonnes of boat and water over a 25-metre height difference in just minutes.(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund is appointed Lord High Executioner and moves a beheading forward from Wednesday to Monday so he and his staff can enjoy some time off. programme.title: Medieval Murder Mysteries
programme.sub-title: King Edward II: A Mysterious Death
programme.desc: The controversy surrounding the death of Edward II, whose forced abdication preceded his mysterious demise while in custody.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: The crimes of Amelia Dyer, who promised mothers that for a fee she would adopt their babies and raise them as her own. In fact, she neglected and murdered countless infants over a 30-year period in Victorian Britain. This episode tells the story of Dyer and of Evelina Marmon, one of the desperate young mothers she deceived.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris attempts to cross the length of Cuba, the only island in the Caribbean to boast an extensive railway network, starting in Havana in the west. This proves to be a major challenge given the unreliability of the service and the old trains regularly used, but makes for a fascinating journey to Chris's ultimate goal, the American naval base at Guantanamo Bay. Along the way he soaks up the sights and sounds of Havana, retraces the footsteps of the country's national hero Che Guevara, and visits the ghost town of Camilo Cienfuegos - a relic from Cuba's colonial sugar plantation era.(n)

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: A polar bear hunts with her cubs, while the warmer weather of spring accelerates a process that will eventually see an area of ice the size of Australia disappear into the Arctic Ocean. A woolly bear caterpillar emerges from the snow after spending the winter frozen, and in Antarctica male Adelie penguins arrive to begin constructing nests in fierce storms as they try to attract mates.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in the desert, including a pride of desert lions that risks hunting a giraffe several times their size. The film also features an immense swarm of locusts, sand grouse flying for 120 miles to gather water for their offspring, and an unprecedented recording of a tiny bat defending itself against one of the world's deadliest scorpions.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the world's fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour.(n)

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The story of the siege of Kohima in 1944, when heavily outnumbered British and Indian troops held off Japanese forces until reinforcements could arrive.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: An account of Hitler's decision to invade the Soviet Union, which produced an initial series of dazzling victories until the harsh winter of 1941 took hold, with fatal consequences for the ill-equipped German army. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.desc: An Albanian village home to Allied secret agents, devastated for resisting the Nazi war machine, and a notorious asylum in America that enforced lobotomies.(n)

With a total of Â£650,000 to spend, and three shots each to find the perfect property - Laura in Berkshire and Jonnie in the ski-resort of Morzine - the pressure is really on for both of them to deliver.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray's efforts to take an interest in one of Ally's hobbies backfire, leaving him searching for a particularly rare trading card to make amends. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Madylin Sweeten and Patricia Heaton.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra finally prepares a palatable meal, much to the surprise of her long-suffering family, who have grown accustomed to a steady stream of culinary disasters.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier fail dismally in their bid to get tickets for a popular new theatre production, while Daphne achieves the dubious distinction of having a photograph of her bottom appear in a Seattle newspaper.(n)

programme.desc: Wendy and Marcus start renovating their chateau's barn into a wedding venue, and Debbie and Simon host a Chinese cooking weekend for their family as a dry run for a new venture.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the coastal community of Long Beach, California, to save an Italian restaurant that was opened in 1958 and now under the misguided leadership of Nino, who blamed by brother Michael and sister Carina for the eatery's failings. Their 78-year-old mother Inge desperate for Nino to turn the business around, but the feuding family members will have to solve their differences first.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys

programme.title: Prue Leith: Journey with My Daughter
programme.desc: The food writer travels to Cambodia with her adopted daughter Li-Da, in the hope of tracking down her biological family. Li-Da was like many of her generation evacuated from the country to escape Pol Pot's genocide, and Prue admits she has rarely questioned whether she could have helped her daughter connect more with her roots. Now, social media and DNA testing are allowing thousands of people to reconnect with lost relatives.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Expats Dave and Sue arrive at the surgery after a 500-mile trip from France to bring their border collie Kaspar, who has a leg pain that is proving hard to diagnose. An eight-year-old pug needs surgery on its elbow, and an Egyptian Mau cat is rushed into Fitzpatrick's after fracturing its knee in a fall from a second-floor balcony. Noel goes ahead with surgery on the complex fracture, but if there are any complications the kitten may face amputation.(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.sub-title: Coronavirus: How Britain Is Changing - Dispatches
programme.desc: As coronavirus takes hold of Britain, Dispatches investigates the long-term effects of the outbreak and asks how the deadly Covid-19 is changing Britain in the long term. As the death toll rises, the programme looks at everything from seismic changes in economic policy to shifts in the way we view and treat other people, and from a drop in pollution to the widening of the gaps between rich and poor, young and old. The programme asks what a post-Covid 19 Britain, torn apart by the disease, might look like, and the potential changes to the way people live their lives for years to come.(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore the Marne-Rhine Canal in Alsace and Lorraine, close to France's border with Germany and running through forests, mountains and valleys. They get to grips with an unfamiliar system of locks, sample local wines, and pay a visit to the European Parliament in Strasbourg.(n)

programme.title: The Mum Who Got Tourette's
programme.desc: Elizabeth is a typical suburban housewife and mother of three with one big difference - without warning, shortly after her 40th birthday, she developed Tourette's syndrome - a neurological condition that causes involuntary tics, some of them offensive. This one-off documentary follows Elizabeth and her family over the course of a busy summer, including a holiday in Cornwall, GCSE results and meeting her son's new girlfriend - experiences amplified by the unexpected impact of Elizabeth's condition.(n)

programme.title: Shut-Ins: Britain's Fattest People
programme.desc: Documentary following the struggle of three severely morbidly obese people as they attempt to regain control of their lives before it is too late. Claire has been bed-bound for the last six years. Trapped by her 40-stone body, she can only see the world through the window of the room where she spends her life. Claire has two daughters - Tracey and Jasmine - who need to do nearly everything for her. Tracey has two young children and a home of her own, so the burden of care falls mainly on 19-year-old Jasmine. The programme tracks the family's journey as local surgeon Nick Carter battles to get Clare the help she needs.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a Â£4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: It's Your Fault I'm Fat

programme.title: On Benefits

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Ever since suffering a brain infection four years ago, Sunderland woman June Findlay has been unable to walk or talk and is totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Since then she has been sleeping on a hospital bed in the kitchen, with no washing or toilet facilities. Joan and John do their best, but Nick Knowles believes they deserve better, so he and his small band of builders - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - set out to extend the Findlays' home, with designer Oliver Heath on hand to protect his vision. Can they give June back her dignity?(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team meet Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah. The couple live in a small 18th-century house in Cumbria, which is currently a half-finished renovation project that had to be put on hold when Noah was born with spina bifida. Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Hereford couple Colin and Clare Gibbons, whose life together fell apart in 2012 when their eldest son Christopher was diagnosed with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, one of the most severe forms of the condition. Despite having a lovely house, it needs adapting to Christopher's needs for both now and in the future, so the DIY SOS team - bolstered by dozens of local tradespeople - descend on the property to add a two-storey extension, with designer Julia Kendell on hand to ensure they stick to her vision while adding a few finishing touches of her own.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Warrington, Cheshire, to meet electrician Dave McCartney, a healthy man until he had a stroke at work that has left him partially paralysed. Now he is forced to sleep in a hospital bed in his kitchen - but it won't be for much longer, as his old workmates team up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to adapt his home to his needs, with designer Charlie Luxton on hand to keep the boys in check.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS

Now he is unable to climb the stairs in his home and has difficulty accessing his bathroom and washing facilities, so designer Gabrielle Blackman teams up with the DIY SOS builders and other members of the local community to work to adapt his home to his needs over the course of just nine days.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Twelve months with the staff and patients of one of Britain's leading cancer hospitals, the Royal Marsden in London. The film follows Rosemary, Phil and Ray as they undergo remarkable new treatments - including a multimillion-pound genetically targeted drug to fight skin cancer and advanced robotic surgery - and reveals the medical breakthroughs that are offering new hope in defeating a disease that more than one in three people will develop at some stage.(n)

programme.title: The Age of Stupid (2008)
programme.desc: Drama documentary examining the impact of climate change. In an environmentally devastated future, the last man alive looks back at footage from 2008 to uncover the reasons why the human race did so little to prevent climate change before it was too late, examining real life stories including a windfarm developer fighting public opposition and an elderly mountain guide who has witnessed the melting of Alpine glaciers first-hand.(n)

programme.title: Burning Men (2018)
programme.desc: Two struggling musicians plan to sell their precious collection of vinyl records to fund a road trip to Memphis. They resort to stealing an extremely rare black metal album, and begin to suspect a curse has been placed on the record as a run of disastrous fortune befalls them. Drama, starring Edward Hayter and Aki Omoshaybi.(n)

programme.title: A Random Encounter (2013)
programme.desc: Two strangers trying to make their way in Hollywood meet by chance in a coffee shop, and come away unable to stop thinking about one another. programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.sub-title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire? Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant invites two pairs of celebrities to play for their favourite charities in this seasonal special. Coronation Street's Antony Cotton and his former co-star Suranne Jones are first up, followed by footballer-turned-presenter Gary Lineker and one-time rugby star Austin Healey, who has more recently been displaying his fancy footwork in Strictly Come Dancing. As usual, 12 questions stand between the celebrities and Â£1million and there are three lifelines available if they need them.(n)
